Creatorship

Legal

Terms of Service

Last updated: March 2025

1. What Creatorship Does

Creatorship is a platform that connects TikTok Shop creators with brands. We use AI to surface creator content to brands, enable licensing of videos for Meta (Facebook/Instagram) advertising, and facilitate commission-based payments. Creators earn when their licensed content drives sales; brands pay a 4% platform fee plus creator commission.

2. Creator Terms

TikTok Access. By connecting your TikTok account, you grant Creatorship read-only access to your public profile (display name, avatar, follower count, video count) and your public video list. We never post, comment, or take any action on your behalf on TikTok.

Deal Approval. When a brand wishes to license your content for Meta ads, you will receive a deal offer. You must approve each deal before your video is used. No content is licensed without your consent.

Payouts. Creator commissions are paid weekly via Stripe. You must provide valid payment details to receive payouts. Payout timing and thresholds are set by Stripe and Creatorship.

3. Brand Terms

Platform Fee. Creatorship charges a 4% fee on revenue generated from ads running licensed creator content. This is in addition to creator commission rates agreed per deal.

Meta Ad Usage Only. Licensed content may only be used to run ads on Meta platforms (Facebook, Instagram). Use on other channels requires separate agreement with the creator.

4. Content Licensing

When you approve a deal, you grant the brand a non-exclusive, worldwide license to use the specified video(s) for Meta advertising during the term of the campaign. You retain ownership of your content. The brand may not edit your content in a materially misleading way without your consent.

5. Payments

All payments are processed through Stripe. Creatorship is not responsible for delays or failures caused by payment processors. Disputes regarding payouts should be directed to Creatorship support.

6. Prohibited Uses

You may not use Creatorship to: violate any law or third-party rights; upload content you do not own or have rights to; impersonate others; distribute malware; or engage in fraud, harassment, or abuse. We may suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.

7. Limitation of Liability

Creatorship is provided "as is." To the maximum extent permitted by law, Creatorship and its affiliates shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or goodwill. Our total liability shall not exceed the greater of the fees you paid to Creatorship in the twelve months prior to the claim or $100.

8. Governing Law

These terms are governed by the laws of the State of South Carolina, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of South Carolina.

Contact: For legal inquiries, email legal@creatorship.app
